evidences.content: # Iran's IRGC Unleashes Massive Hormuz Strait Drills Before U.S Nuke Talks | Trump Khamenei | 4K ## CNN-News18 10800000 subscribers 40 likes ### Description 3403 views Posted: 17 Feb 2026 Iran's Revolutionary Guards launched the "Smart Control of Hormuz Strait" military drill on February 16, 2026, in the critical oil chokepoint just one day before US nuclear talks, where senior officials including IRGC Commander Maj.-Gen. Mohammad Pakpour oversaw numerous strikes causing huge explosions to test naval readiness against security threats. The exercise assessed operational forces' preparedness for reciprocal actions amid sky-high US-Iran tensions. Tehran flexed its muscle in the world's most vital sea passage, signaling no fear of confrontation.

evidences.content: ## Incidents in the oil chokepoint In 1984, during the Iran-Iraq War, the feuding Persian Gulf countries began attacking each other’s oil-tanker shipping and Iran also attacked tankers heading to and from Kuwait and other Gulf states. The so-called Tanker War, which included strikes on more than 100 oil tankers, prompted the involvement of the United States and several western European nations to ensure the safe passage of oil tankers in and out of the Gulf. In the 21st century Iranian and U.S. naval forces have engaged in several standoffs in the strait. [...] Meanwhile, retaliatory missile and drone strikes by Iran embroiled the region. They followed a familiar pattern of horizontal escalation, according to the international security scholar Robert A. Pape writing in _Foreign Affairs_. Iran’s war strategy widened the arena of conflict, thereby extending the conflict beyond mere military might and into the political and economic realms, with the aim of withstanding bombardment until the conflict would become too costly for the United States and Israel to be willing to sustain. Iranian strikes targeted U.S. embassies and military installations in the United Arab Emirates, Saudi...
